description: Golden English→Fonoran regression corpus (stranger-first, 1,000 phrases). Each phrase has expected `fon` where committed; honest gaps tracked in gap baseline. Source: external/fonora-data/data/fonoran-stranger-corpus.json

phrases.en: I do not understand your words.
I will not move toward you.
I am glad you are here.
I will say my name slowly.
Are you a person like me?
I am a stranger and I need help.
I am lost and I am alone.
Do you want me to move back?
I feel relieved to see you.
You do not need to be afraid.
I do not know your language.
Can we try to understand each other?
Point to yourself and say your name.
Are there other people near you?
We are in the same place now.
I will find food for us.
We need to find shelter now.
I will keep the fire burning.
I need clean water for this wound.
Please press here to stop the bleeding.
I am sick and very tired.
The wound needs to be covered.
This plant may help the wound.
Do you have cloth for the wound?
Let me look at your injury.
I fell and hit my head.
I am cold and I feel sick.
I cannot carry that, I am hurt.
I will help clean your wound.
This area is red and swollen.
Did you mean this place hurts?
Show me where the pain is.
Is the pain here or there?
I do not understand, show me again.
My back hurts and I cannot stand.
I am very afraid right now.
Is it safe to go there?
I see tracks on the ground.
Do not go near that animal.
Stay low and do not speak.
There is a large animal ahead.
We need to leave this area.
I see something in the dark.
I do not feel safe here.
We can rest here, it is safe.
I heard a loud noise nearby.
Wait here and do not move.
I think I said danger wrong.
Is there an animal near the water?
I am afraid and I want to leave.
That animal is fast and large.
We ran and now we are safe.
I was worried, but I am calm now.
I want you to be calm.
I want you to be happy.
You do not need to be sad.
I was angry, but I am calm now.
I do not want to be alone.
I see that you are sad.
I am angry but not at you.
I did not mean to hurt you.
I will not leave you alone.
I am thankful for your help.
I am sorry I took that.
I did not want to fight.
I am glad we are together.
I am not angry with you.
Let me carry that for you.
I am cold and I need help.
We survived and I am grateful.
Can I help you with that?
I promise I will not leave you.
I need you to stop now.
Do not look at my things.
I am angry and I need quiet.
This side is mine and that side is yours.
I feel scared when you come close.
I need you to move back.
I am tired and I need rest.
What is that noise at night?
What is that mark on your arm?
I do not know what this is.
I do not know what that is.
What is the name of this?
What do you see over there?
I want to know what this is.
What do you want me to do?
I do not understand what you want.
What is that over the water?
What is safe to eat here?
I am not sure what that is.
What is that on the ground?
I want to know what happened.
I feel afraid and I do not know what is there.
What do you want to show me?
What is that color in the sky?
Please show me what you mean.
I see something and I do not know what it is.
What do you call this thing?
What is that thing over there?
That person is a stranger to me.
I do not recognize that person.
I feel afraid of that person.
That person is alone and looks lost.
Please tell me who you are.
I am confused about who is here.
I do not understand who that is.
That person is cold and shaking.
I feel uneasy about that person.
Who is the youngest person here?
I want to know who you are.
Tell me your name if you can.
I feel glad you are here.
Who is the strongest person here?
That person is a friend or an enemy.
I did not understand who you meant.
That person is hurt and needs us.
Who is that person over there?
I do not know where we are.
The fire is close to us.
I slept near the big rock.
Point to where the water is.
The path goes to the left.
Is the village far from here?
The path is behind those trees.
Show me where the food is.
I am scared of this place.
The high ground is that way.
Let us go toward the light.
Is there water in that direction?
I do not see the path.
Stay here and wait for me.
The old person is near the water.
I am not sure which way.
The sun is in that direction.
Can you see water from here?
The water and the food are there.
I am relieved to find water.
The child is near the big tree.
Come here and look at this.
I meant that place, not this one.
I am worried and I feel lost.
The dry land is to the right.
We go left and then up.
Is it time to eat now?
After the rain, we can move.
Is it morning or night now?
We should rest before we walk.
Is it time to go now?
Before we sleep, we need water.
When does the sun rise here?
I was afraid before, not now.
After we eat, we can rest.
Is it too late to move?
When did you last drink water?
Can we leave after the rain?
Not yet, wait a little more.
When will it be light again?
We move now and rest later.
After you sleep, I will watch.
Is it safe to go now?
We should eat before we walk.
Soon the sun will go down.
I mean later, not right now.
After I rest, I will help.
When will the water come back?
I felt safe before, not now.
Let us decide later, not now.
Before we go, drink some water.
I am going to the water.
I feel afraid to go there.
I do not want to go alone.
I am going and coming back.
Do you want to follow me?
I will stay and wait here.
I am scared and I want to stay.
I will go and you stay.
Are you going to come back?
I did not follow you on purpose.
I feel relieved that you came back.
Do not move from that spot.
You left and I was alone.
I meant to go the other way.
I feel tired but I must keep moving.
Are we moving toward the water?
I stayed and waited for a long time.
I will return this to you.
Do you have more of this?
I will give you this rope.
I have a knife and a rope.
Do you want to share this?
I do not have enough water.
I feel uneasy taking your food.
I have water and you have food.
Can I take one of those?
I will return your container soon.
I feel good giving this to you.
I have a small amount of this.
Give me the large piece and take the small one.
I want to exchange this for that.
I feel worried that I have too little.
Can you give me a little water?
I will share my shelter with you.
Take this and give me that one.
Do you have a container I can borrow?
I am sorry, I took the wrong thing.
We each have enough food now.
I feel relieved that we can share.
I think I took yours by mistake.
You can borrow this but please return it.
I will give you some of this.
I do not eat that animal.
I tasted it and it is safe.
Do not eat that red berry.
This root is good to eat.
I do not know this food.
Do you want to eat now?
Is it going to rain more?
We must stay near the fire.
Do you see the dark clouds?
I am afraid of the storm.
We need dry wood for fire.
It is too dark to move.
The ground is wet and cold.
Is it cold where you are?
The storm is loud and strong.
We must not let the fire die.
I do not understand the weather here.
The air is cold and wet.
Please move away from the wind.
Is it safe to go outside now?
The wind is cold and sharp.
We need shelter from the rain.
I do not know this word.
The night is long and cold.
I feel better near the fire.
Give me wood for the fire.
The darkness is frightening to me.
The rain and wind came together.
It is raining very hard now.
The night will be very cold.
I need to breathe fresh air.
I feel pain in my side.
I am tired but not sick.
I could not sleep last night.
I am looking for my father.
I am worried about my child.
The child is cold and scared.
Can you help the old person?
The baby is not breathing well.
I am the mother of this child.
The old person cannot walk far.
I feel afraid for my baby.
Do you have a mother here?
Give food to the child first.
My mother is old and tired.
I feel sad without my family.
The old person is in pain.
Is the baby a girl or boy?
The child is tired and hungry.
I need to find my mother.
I feel alone without my child.
I am frightened for my family.
The child needs warmth and food.
That is not what I meant.
Please say it one more time.
Do you mean the big one?
Do you mean this or that?
That word is not clear to me.
I do not understand this signal.
Please do that one more time.
That is not what I wanted.
Please show me the correct one.
I do not know that word.
Did you mean the small one?
I do not understand and I am sorry.
I was wrong and I will try again.
We do not understand each other yet.
Let us start that part again.
I want to leave this place.
Can you help me carry this?
Maybe we can find water tomorrow.
I will try to start a fire.
Do you want to move now?
I hope someone finds us soon.
We can sleep here and leave at light.
I plan to go up that hill.
Can you try to climb that tree?
I want food and I want rest.
Will you stay here with me?
I can carry that for you.
We will try to find the path.
Do you plan to go that way?
I hope we find shelter before dark.
I want to go toward the water.
Can we rest for a short time?
I will not go there alone.
Do you want to come with me?
I think I can fix this.
We will need more wood for the fire.
Can you try to signal for help?
I hope the rain stops soon.
Maybe we can share the work.
I want to try a different path.
Will you watch while I sleep?
I plan to gather food in the morning.
Can you show me what you plan?
I will try and I will not stop.
We can go together or stay here.
I want to understand your plan.
Maybe we can build a shelter today.
Can we make a plan together?
I hope we can find each other again.
Will you try to find more food?
I want to rest and then move.
Maybe that path leads to people.
I can try to make a signal.
Do you want to stay or go?
I will wait here for you.
I hope this is the right way.
Can you wait for me here?
I plan to watch through the night.
Maybe we can try a different way.
Will we find people near the water?
I want to find higher ground.
I am grateful for your help.
Thank you for staying with me.
I am going to sleep now.
We have enough food and water.
Is this a good place to stop?
That was hard but we finished.
I will not forget your help.
We can rest and then continue.
Do you want to stop here?
This is a good place to sleep.
I am glad this day is over.
You kept me warm and safe.
I will stay near you tonight.
We are both alive and well.
Thank you for the food today.
Is there more to do now?
I see you and I am grateful.
You were good to me today.
I feel calm and at peace.
You did not leave me alone.
Are you going to sleep now?
I will come back to this place.
We made it through this day.
